chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D-1397 of 2023 (Mst. Reema and another Vs. Province of Sindh & others) DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For Orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of CMA No. 5906/2023 (Stay) 3. For hearing of main case. 28-02-2024. Mr. Muhammad Qayyum Arain, advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Shaharyar Imdad Awan, Assistant A.G, Sindh. ******** 1. Over ruled. 2&3. By way of instant petition, it is stated by the petitioners that they have qualified their written test for their appointment as police constables; therefore, the respondents to be directed to conduct their interview and appoint them accordingly. As per the comments filed by Senior Superintendent Police, Sukkur the names of the petitioners are not mentioned in the final approval/selected list. If it is so then it prima-facie suggests that the petitioners on having been interviewed have not qualified for their appointment as police constables. Be that as it may, the dispute with regard to the fact that the petitioners have qualified written test or interview for their appointment as police constable or otherwise is involving the factual controversy, which could not be resolved by this Court in exercise of its Constitutional jurisdiction; consequently the instant petition being misconceived is dismissed accordingly. Judge Judge Nasim/P.A
